reason: Despite significant institutional inflows into Bitcoin ETFs, the price has struggled to sustain upward momentum above $71,000. This suggests robust selling pressure or profit-taking is absorbing demand, a characteristic of a distribution phase. While Bitcoin has shown remarkable resilience to geopolitical turmoil and outperformed traditional assets and altcoins, this supply/demand imbalance at elevated price levels, coupled with ongoing macro risks, warrants caution.
headlines: Bitcoin jumps above $71,000, building on its resilience to Middle East conflict
Ray Dalio says 'there is only one gold' as bitcoin outperforms gold during worst geopolitical crisis in years
Over a billion flows into bitcoin ETFs, yet the price isn’t rising — an analyst explains why
Bitcoin holds, ether, solana slide as Mideast woes drag Asian equities to multi-year lows
Ripple expands payments platform into end-to-end stablecoin infrastructure as processed volume tops $100 billion
